question: Tell me about a time when you couldn't keep an appointment with your family or friends. What happened, and how did you deal with the situation?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had to cancel dinner plans with a close friend, and I still remember it clearly. We were supposed to meet at a nice restaurant, so I was really excited about catching up. Actually, that afternoon, I suddenly came down with a really bad cold, and I felt so weak that I could barely get out of bed. I called my friend right away to explain the situation, but she had already left her house and was on her way. Honestly, I felt terrible about making her change her plans at the last minute. And then, she said she understood and told me not to worry, which made me feel a little better. In the end, we just rescheduled and met up a few days later. So yeah, this experience taught me to always let people know as soon as possible if I can’t make it, and it’s still one of those memories that reminds me to be more thoughtful.

question: Sometimes, funny or surprising things happen during an appointment. Tell me about an appointment that you remember for some reason. Tell me everything that happened and why it was so memorable.
answer: Well, a while back, I had a dentist appointment that really stood out to me because something pretty funny happened. I went there with a small toothache, so I was a little nervous while I waited in the quiet clinic. Actually, there were only a few people around and the atmosphere felt calm, which made me even more focused on my worries. And then, the dentist came out to call the next patient, and I realized he was an old friend from high school that I hadn't seen in years. We both just burst out laughing when we recognized each other, and it totally broke the ice. Honestly, after that, I felt way more relaxed and comfortable about the appointment. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories because a simple dentist visit turned into a fun reunion with an old friend.

question: Sometimes, problems arise when dealing with the bank. Tell me in detail about a problem you had that involved your bank. Tell me the story of a time when you had to solve a problem involving your bank.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a problem at my bank that really stressed me out. I went there with the goal of sending some money for an important payment. When I arrived, the bank was packed with people, and actually, the whole system was down, so none of the machines were working at all. Because of that, everyone, including me, had to wait for almost an hour, and the atmosphere was pretty tense. And then, while I was waiting, I noticed that the staff were doing their best to help everyone, which made things a little better. Honestly, I felt pretty annoyed at first, but after seeing how kind and patient the staff were, I started to calm down. In the end, one of the employees helped me finish everything quickly when the system came back up. So yeah, it's still one of my most memorable experiences at a bank because it taught me to be patient when problems come up.

question: Have you ever had any problems with your bank card when you were using an ATM? Perhaps your card stuck in the machine. Describe the problem you had in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a pretty scary experience with my bank card at an ATM. I went to the machine late at night by myself because I really needed some cash for an emergency. Actually, the street was almost empty and the area felt a little unsafe, so I was already kind of on edge. I put my card in, but suddenly the machine froze and my card got stuck inside. I tried pressing every button I could find, but nothing worked and the screen just stayed frozen. Honestly, I felt really panicked at that moment because it was my only card and I didn’t know what to do. In the end, I called the bank’s emergency number, and they managed to get my card back to me the next morning. So yeah, it’s still one of my most memorable and stressful experiences with an ATM.

question: Can you remember an experience you had with a bank account, a credit card, or an ATM card that required some assistance? Perhaps you lost your card. Tell me about that experience in as much detail as you can.
answer: Well, a while back, I lost my credit card while I was out having lunch with a friend because I must have dropped it somewhere without noticing. Actually, I realized it was missing when I tried to pay for our meal, and I started to panic since I couldn't remember where I had last seen it. The restaurant was pretty busy that day, so I felt even more anxious as I searched my bag and pockets but couldn't find the card anywhere. And then, I quickly called the card company to report the loss and asked them to block my card right away. The person on the phone was really calm and helpful, and they guided me through the whole process step by step. Honestly, I felt so relieved after talking to them because they made everything much easier. In the end, they sent me a new card just a few days later, and it's still one of my most memorable experiences because it taught me to always check my wallet carefully.

question: Now, tell me about a time when you had a problem while using a bike or any other type of transportation. What happened and how did you deal with the problem?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a problem with my bike that I still remember clearly. I was riding to meet a friend on a sunny weekend, and the road was pretty quiet, so I just enjoyed the ride. Actually, everything was going smoothly until my back tire suddenly went flat, and I almost lost my balance. Because of that, I had to get off and push my bike all the way to a repair shop, which was kind of embarrassing since people kept looking at me. And then, the shop fixed my tire really quickly, but I ended up being a bit late to meet my friend. Honestly, I felt so tired and a little embarrassed walking my bike like that, but I was also relieved when it was fixed. So yeah, even though it was a hassle at the time, it's still one of my favorite funny memories whenever I think about it.

question: Has anything interesting or unusual ever happened in the area which you live? Tell me in detail about the memorable event that has happened in your area.
answer: Well, a while back, something really exciting happened in my neighborhood with my family. The city organized a big street festival right near my house because it was a special weekend event. Actually, the whole area was covered with colorful lights, and it was packed with food stalls and people enjoying themselves. We walked down together, tried lots of different street food, and even watched a live concert that was set up in the middle of the street. And then, we spent hours just walking around, talking, and soaking up the lively atmosphere. Honestly, I felt so happy and relaxed that night because everyone seemed to be having a great time. So yeah, it’s still one of my favorite memories because it brought my family and neighbors together in such a fun way.

question: Have you ever bought a piece of clothing that you did not like after you bought it? Tell me the story of a time when you bought a new piece of clothing that had some kind of problem. What did you do to solve the problem?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I bought a new jacket online because it looked really stylish in the photos, and I wanted something nice for the winter. When the package arrived, I opened it right away, but actually, I noticed there was a small rip on one of the sleeves. I felt pretty disappointed because I had waited about a week for it to come, and I was really looking forward to wearing it. And then, I took some photos of the damage and contacted the shop’s customer service to explain the problem. Honestly, I was a little worried that they wouldn't help me or that it would be a hassle to get a replacement. In the end, the staff were very friendly and quickly sent me a new jacket for free, which made me feel relieved. So yeah, it’s still one of those memories that reminds me to check things carefully whenever I shop online.

question: Have you ever bought a piece of clothing and found out that there was a problem with it? Tell me what the problem was and what you did to solve the situation.
answer: Well, a while back, I bought a shirt at a store near my house because I needed something to wear for a special event. Actually, I was in a bit of a rush that day, so I just grabbed the shirt and paid without checking it carefully. When I got home and looked at it again, I noticed there was a small dark stain near the collar, which really surprised me. The store was usually pretty good, so I hadn't expected any problems. And then, I decided to go back to the store the next day with my receipt and show them the stain. The staff apologized for the mistake and exchanged the shirt for a new one right away, which was a relief. Honestly, I felt a little embarrassed for not checking it before buying, but I was glad the problem was solved so easily. So yeah, it's still one of those small memories that stuck with me whenever I buy clothes now.

question: Have you ever had an unsatisfactory experience at a coffee shop? Describe what happened, why it didn't meet your expectations, and how you handled it.
answer: Well, a while back, I had an unsatisfactory experience at a coffee shop with a close friend. We went there because we wanted to catch up and talk for a while. When we arrived, the place was really crowded and noisy, so we waited a long time just to order our drinks. Actually, when our drinks finally arrived, mine was completely wrong and tasted super bitter, which was disappointing after waiting so long. And then, I decided to let the staff know about the mistake, so I explained the situation politely. Honestly, I felt pretty annoyed at first, but I tried to stay calm because I knew mistakes could happen. In the end, the staff quickly made me a new drink and even gave me a small cookie as an apology. So yeah, even though the visit started badly, it's still a memorable experience for me because it taught me to be patient and understanding in these situations.

question: Tell me about one of the memorable experiences you had at a dentist office. What happened from beginning to end? Tell me everything in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a really memorable experience at a dentist's office with my mom because I had a terrible toothache that wouldn't let me sleep. The clinic was actually very clean and quiet, but I was feeling pretty nervous when I got there. And then, I sat down in the dentist's chair and the dentist explained the whole process to me in a calm voice, which helped a little. He started working on my tooth, and honestly, I was scared at first, but he was so gentle and careful that I relaxed pretty quickly. The treatment didn't take long at all, and before I knew it, the pain was completely gone. Personally, I felt so relieved and grateful as I left the clinic that day. So yeah, it's still one of my most unforgettable memories because I overcame my fear and felt so much better afterwards.

question: Talk about a time when you visited a friend or a family member. What did you do when you visited them? What was memorable about that visit? Tell me everything from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a while back, I visited my grandparents with my parents because it had been such a long time since we last saw them. When we arrived, their house was pretty old but it felt really warm and cozy, which made me feel welcome right away. Actually, my grandmother cooked a huge dinner for us, and we all sat together around the table, sharing stories and catching up. And then, after dinner, the kids ran around outside while the adults kept talking and laughing inside. I remember we spent hours just enjoying each other's company, and time went by so quickly. Honestly, I felt so happy and comfortable during that visit, surrounded by my family. In the end, it turned out to be a really special day for all of us. It's still one of my favorite memories, and I'll never forget how good it felt to be together like that.

question: Could you tell me about a special event or a holiday you celebrated with your family or friends? Tell me all about that event or the holiday from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I celebrated my grandfather's birthday with my whole family at his house because it was a really big birthday for him. The house was packed with relatives, and the atmosphere was warm and lively. Actually, we all worked together to cook a huge meal, and everyone helped out in the kitchen, laughing and talking as we prepared the food. And then, after dinner, we brought out a cake with candles, sang happy birthday to my grandfather, and took lots of family photos. He looked so happy and touched, and everyone cheered for him. Honestly, I felt really close to my family that day, and I was just grateful to spend such a meaningful time together. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories because it brought all of us together and made my grandfather feel really special.

question: Tell me how you found out about eating healthy. Did your family eat healthy when you were growing up? Describe in detail how you started to eat healthy.
answer: Well, a while back, I really started to care about eating healthy, and I still remember how it all began. At that time, I often got sick and felt tired almost every day because I usually ate a lot of fast food. Actually, my family didn't pay much attention to healthy eating when I was growing up, so I just followed their habits. But one day, a close friend noticed how I looked and suggested that I try eating more fruits and vegetables. I decided to listen to her advice, so I slowly started to buy healthier food at the store and cooked simple meals at home. And then, after a few weeks, I noticed that I felt much better and had more energy. Honestly, I was surprised by how big a difference it made in my daily life. So yeah, that small change really changed my habits, and it's still one of my most important memories about health.

question: Tell me about a memorable experience you had while eating something. It could have been something good or bad. What happened? Why was it so memorable or special?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a really memorable meal while I was traveling with my family. We went to a small local restaurant because we were all starving after a long, tiring day of sightseeing. Actually, the place was pretty tiny and simple, but it smelled so good as soon as we walked in, so we felt excited to try something new. We looked at the menu and ordered a dish that none of us had ever tasted before. And then, when the food finally came out, we all took our first bites and just stopped talking because it tasted amazing. Honestly, I felt so happy in that moment, sharing delicious food with my family after such a busy day. We ended up ordering the same dish again because everyone loved it so much. So yeah, that meal really became the highlight of our whole trip, and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Sometimes when we plan a meal, things do not work out. Tell me a story of a time when a plan for a meal did not work out as planned for you or someone you know.
answer: Well, a while back, I planned a small gathering with some friends at my place, and I ordered food online because I wanted everything to be easy. At that time, I was busy cleaning up the house and getting things ready, so I just trusted that the food would arrive on time. Actually, as the evening went on, an hour passed and nothing showed up, so I started to worry. I called the restaurant, and they told me they had lost my order, which really surprised me. And then, my friends arrived and I had no food to serve them, so I felt pretty stressed and embarrassed. Honestly, I just decided to make some quick pasta with whatever I had in the kitchen, and we ended up laughing about the whole situation together. In the end, it turned into a really fun night, and it's still one of my favorite memories because we made the best of it.

question: Tell me about how you found a food store. Where is it located and why do you like going there? What do you like to shop for there?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I found a little food store near my house when I took a different way home after work. Actually, I was just walking alone and the bright sign suddenly caught my attention, so I decided to go inside. The store was pretty small, but it was full of fresh fruit and tasty snacks everywhere. And then, I looked around and saw that everything looked really clean and well-organized, which made a good impression on me. I picked out some apples and a few healthy snacks that I hadn't tried before, and the owner was really friendly, so I ended up chatting with her for a bit. Honestly, I felt happy and comfortable there because the atmosphere was so nice and welcoming. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories because I found that store by accident, and it became my go-to place for groceries after that.

question: Sometimes, problems arise when it comes to furniture. Tell me about a time when you had a problem with a piece of furniture. Tell me everything that happened and how you fixed the problem.
answer: Well, a while back, I had a problem with a chair I bought online for my desk, and I still remember how it all happened. I ordered it because I needed a comfortable chair to study, but when it arrived, one of the legs was loose and a bit wobbly. Actually, I checked the box and realized that a screw was missing, which explained the problem. The chair was brand new, so I felt really disappointed and a little frustrated since I had been looking forward to using it right away. And then, I decided to contact the shop and explained the situation to them. They responded quickly and sent me the missing screw within a few days. Personally, I was relieved and pretty happy when I finally fixed the chair myself and saw that it was stable. So yeah, it’s still a memorable experience for me because it taught me to always check everything carefully when I buy new furniture.

question: Have you ever bought furniture that wasn't what you had expected? Maybe you got the wrong color or maybe it didn't fit in the space. Tell me about a time when you had a problem with a new piece of furniture.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I bought a small table for my room and expected it to fit perfectly. I ordered it online because the pictures made it look just right for my space. Actually, I didn't measure my room at all back then, so I just guessed the size would work. When the table arrived, it turned out to be way too big for the corner I had in mind. The room was already a bit crowded, so I had a hard time finding a good spot for it. And then, I ended up moving some other furniture around just to make everything fit. Honestly, I felt pretty frustrated and a little silly for not checking the measurements first. In the end, that experience taught me to always measure my space before buying anything new, and it's still one of my most memorable shopping mistakes.

question: Have you ever bought something for your home or yourself that did not work out? Tell me everything that happened and how you eventually solved the problem.
answer: Well, a while back, I bought a lamp for my room because my old one had finally broken. I was in a hurry that day, so I just picked the first nice lamp I saw at the store. Actually, the store was pretty busy, and I didn't really check it carefully. When I got home and plugged it in, the light kept flickering on and off, which was really frustrating. Honestly, I felt pretty annoyed because I had just spent money on it and needed it right away. And then, I decided to take it back to the store the next day and explained the problem to the staff. In the end, they checked it and saw it was broken, so they gave me a new one without any trouble. So yeah, it's still a good memory for me because it reminds me to take my time and check things carefully before buying them.

question: Tell me about a time when you helped with a party or a celebration. Perhaps you helped invite people, or decorate, or get the food. Tell me about this experience from beginning to end in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I helped organize a surprise birthday party for a close friend because we wanted to do something special for her. I was with a small group of friends, and we decided to keep everything a secret. Actually, I was in charge of decorating the room, so I spent the whole afternoon blowing up balloons and hanging up banners while my friends prepared the food. The place was pretty quiet at first, but as we got closer to the party time, everyone started to get excited and a little nervous. Honestly, I felt really anxious that our friend might find out about the surprise before she arrived. And then, when she finally walked in, she looked so shocked and even started to cry because she was so happy. In the end, the party was a huge success and everyone enjoyed themselves. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories because I loved seeing how much it meant to her.

question: Sometimes when people get together for celebrations or gatherings, unexpected things can happen. Tell me all about something unexpected that happened at a celebration or a gathering in the area where you live.
answer: Well, a while back, I had something really unexpected happen at a family gathering. I was with all my relatives at my parents' house because it was a holiday, and the place was packed and full of energy. Actually, just as we were about to start eating, the power suddenly went out in the whole building, so everything got really dark and quiet. At first, everyone was a bit confused and surprised, but then we all started laughing and tried to make the best of it. And then, we found some candles, lit them, and continued our dinner by candlelight, which made the whole evening feel extra cozy and special. Honestly, I felt really happy because it turned a normal meal into a unique and memorable experience. In the end, the power came back after about an hour, but we all agreed that the candlelit dinner was the best part. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories from a family gathering.

question: Tell me about a gathering or a celebration that was held in the area in which you live. Describe where the gathering was held, who was there, what the purpose was, and what happened from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a few years ago, my neighborhood held a community gathering at a small park near my house because it was a local holiday. The park was decorated with colorful banners and balloons, and many neighbors and families showed up with homemade food. Actually, the atmosphere was really lively, and everyone seemed excited to be there. We started by sharing food and chatting, and then some people played music while others organized games for both kids and adults. The children ran around the park laughing, and the adults joined in some fun activities too. Honestly, I felt so connected to everyone in my area that day, and it was really heartwarming to see everyone enjoying themselves together. In the end, it turned out to be a memorable and friendly event, and I met a lot of new neighbors. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories from my neighborhood.

question: People often have memorable and moving experiences when they explore their country's geography. Tell me the story of a memorable time you had while visiting a natural place in your country.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went to a beautiful mountain with my family because we wanted to enjoy the autumn scenery together. The whole mountain was covered in bright red and orange leaves, so it was really breathtaking to see. Actually, we took our time hiking up the trail, stopping often to take pictures and just take in the view. And then, when we finally reached the top, we all sat down and shared some snacks while looking out over the amazing landscape. Honestly, I felt so peaceful and relaxed at that moment, just being surrounded by nature and my loved ones. We laughed and talked for a while, and I remember thinking I wished that day could last longer. In the end, it was such a refreshing and special experience for all of us. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories whenever I think about spending time in nature with my family.

question: Have you ever experienced any inconvenience because of a broken or malfunctioning appliance? Describe what happened, how it affected you, and how you resolved the situation.
answer: Well, a while back, my refrigerator broke down, and I was at home with my family when it happened because it was the middle of summer and the weather was really hot. Actually, the fridge just stopped staying cold all of a sudden, so a lot of our food started going bad really quickly. The kitchen was filled with a strange smell, and we had to throw away almost everything inside, which felt like a huge waste. And then, I tried to look up some solutions online, but I couldn’t figure out how to fix it myself. Honestly, I felt pretty frustrated and helpless at the time because I didn’t know what to do. So I called a repair person, and he came over the next morning and fixed the problem in about an hour. Overall, it was a really stressful experience, but it’s still one of those memories that sticks with me whenever I open the fridge now.

question: Tell me about a memorable experience related to getting a haircut. What happened? Why was it memorable? How did you feel about it?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I got a haircut that really stood out to me. I went to a new salon by myself because I wanted to try a totally different style. Actually, the place was bright and modern, so I felt pretty comfortable when I walked in. I asked the stylist to cut my hair much shorter than usual, and then she started working. Honestly, I was a little nervous while she was cutting it, since it was such a big change and there was no going back. But when she finished, I looked in the mirror and I actually loved the new look. After that, people around me kept saying it looked great, which made me feel even better. So yeah, that haircut gave me a lot of confidence, and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Tell me about a time when you were not satisfied with your hairstyle after visiting a salon. What was wrong, and how did you handle the situation?
answer: Well, a while back, I went to a new hair salon because my regular place was fully booked. I just wanted a small trim, so I explained exactly what I wanted to the stylist. Actually, the salon was pretty busy that day, and the stylist seemed a little rushed. When she finished, I saw that my hair was cut way too short—almost twice as much as I had asked for. Honestly, I felt really upset and disappointed when I looked in the mirror because it was not what I expected at all. I told the stylist calmly that I was unhappy with the cut, and she apologized right away. And then, she tried her best to fix the shape and even gave me a discount for the trouble. So yeah, it was a tough experience, but it's still a memory that reminds me to be extra clear about what I want at the salon.

question: Tell me about a time when you or someone you know did something new to become healthier. Maybe it was playing a sport or eating healthier food. Tell me about this change in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I decided to start running to get healthier because I always felt tired and out of shape. At that time, I could barely run for five minutes without stopping, so I felt a little embarrassed at first. Actually, I began by just jogging around my neighborhood a few times each week, and the first week was so hard that I almost gave up. And then, as the days went by, it slowly got easier and I started to enjoy it more. Honestly, I felt really proud of myself because I kept going even when it was tough. In the end, running became one of my favorite habits and I felt much stronger and healthier. So yeah, it's still one of my best memories because that change really improved my life.

question: Tell me about a specific incident related to being healthy that happened to you. Maybe you tried to cook a healthy dish or began an exercise program. Tell me the story from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a while back, I tried cooking a healthy meal for the first time by myself because I wanted to stop eating out so much and start taking better care of my health. At that point, I really couldn't cook at all, so it was a pretty big challenge for me. Actually, I picked out a simple recipe online and went to the store to buy all kinds of fresh vegetables. When I got home, I started chopping and cooking, but the kitchen quickly turned into a mess, and I almost burned the food. And then, even though things got a little crazy, the meal ended up tasting okay, and I was really proud of myself. Honestly, I felt pretty happy and motivated to try cooking again after that. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories because that first messy meal pushed me to eat healthier and learn a new skill.

question: Tell me about a specific time when you learned something about how to maintain good health. It might have been a class you took, or a book you read. Describe what happened from beginning to end in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I read a book about health when I kept feeling really tired all the time, even though I got enough sleep. I was by myself, and honestly, I didn't know much about how to take care of my body back then. Actually, I decided to read it every night before bed because I wanted to feel better. The book was pretty easy to understand, and it explained some simple ways to stay healthy, like drinking more water and going to bed earlier. And then, I started following those tips right away, and after just a few weeks, I noticed I had much more energy during the day. Personally, I felt really proud of myself for making those changes, even though they seemed small. So yeah, learning from that book really changed my daily habits, and it's still one of my favorite memories because it made such a big difference in my life.

question: Tell me about something you have done to try to improve your health. Tell me why you chose this particular activity and how it all worked out.
answer: Well, a while back, I joined a yoga class to try to improve my health. I did it by myself because I wanted to make a change in my daily routine. I picked yoga because I was looking for something calm and gentle, not a really hard workout. At that time, my body was so stiff that I could barely touch my toes, so I thought yoga might help. Actually, the first class was pretty tough and I felt a bit awkward since I was new to it. And then, after a few weeks, I noticed that I became much more flexible and I also felt less stressed. Honestly, I was really surprised by how much better I felt both physically and mentally. So yeah, yoga made a big difference for me, and it's still one of my favorite memories when I think about improving my health.

question: Tell me about a memorable meal you had. Who were you with and where did you have the meal? What kind of food did you have?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a really memorable meal with my friends at a small barbecue place because we wanted to celebrate the end of a long week. The restaurant was pretty cozy and a little crowded, but the smell of grilled meat filled the air and made us even hungrier. Actually, we ordered way too much food and just kept eating and talking for hours. And then, we started telling funny stories, and we laughed so hard that my stomach actually hurt. Personally, I felt so happy and relaxed that night because I could really forget about my worries and just enjoy the moment. In the end, that dinner became one of our favorite memories together, and it still brings a smile to my face when I think about it now.

question: Maintaining one's health can be a challenge. Tell me in detail about a particular challenge you faced related to your health. Tell me everything you did to face that challenge.
answer: Well, a while back, I tried to cut out sugar from my diet because I realized I was eating way too many sweets every single day. At that time, I usually had dessert after almost every meal, so it was a tough habit to break. Actually, I decided to slowly replace candy and soda with fruit and water instead of quitting all at once. And then, during the first few days, it was so difficult that I almost gave up because I kept thinking about sweets. Honestly, I felt really frustrated and tired at first, but after about a week, my cravings got much weaker and I started to feel lighter and healthier. In the end, I even slept better than before and I was really proud of myself for sticking with it. So yeah, beating that habit felt like a big win and it's still one of my favorite memories about taking care of my health.

question: Describe a specific time when you did something to improve your health. It could be the first day that you began a diet, a training program at the gym or a new sport.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I joined a gym for the first time with the goal of getting healthier and feeling stronger. I decided to sign up because I wanted to be more active, and I knew I needed to make a change in my life. When I walked in, the gym was huge and a little overwhelming, and I noticed all the machines that I had never used before. Actually, a friendly trainer came up to me and offered to show me how to use some of the equipment, which really helped me relax. And then, I tried a few simple exercises that the trainer recommended, like using the treadmill and lifting some light weights. Honestly, I was pretty nervous and shy at first because there were so many people around, but after I finished my first workout, I felt really refreshed and proud of myself. So yeah, that first day at the gym made a big difference for me, and it's still one of my favorite memories about improving my health.

question: Have you ever had a memorable experience while hiking? Describe what happened, who you were with, and any challenges you encountered along the way.
answer: Well, a while back, I went hiking with a friend, and it turned into one of my most memorable experiences. We picked a small mountain because the weather seemed perfect that morning. The trail was full of green trees, and the air was really fresh, so we felt excited as we started our climb. Actually, about halfway up the mountain, it suddenly began to rain really hard. We hadn’t brought any rain gear, so we got pretty soaked and a little cold. And then, instead of turning back, we found a small shelter and waited together for the rain to stop. Honestly, I felt a bit worried at first, but it also became kind of fun because we just laughed and talked while we waited. In the end, the rain stopped, and the view from the top was absolutely beautiful, so yeah, it’s still one of my favorite memories from hiking.

question: Have you ever had a holiday plan that did not go as you expected? Maybe there was unusual weather. Tell me about the holiday that did not go the way you planned and what you did to deal with the situation.
answer: Well, a few years ago, my family and I planned a trip to the beach because it was a long holiday weekend. We were all really excited and had already packed our bags for the trip. Actually, the night before we were supposed to leave, a huge storm hit our area and all the roads got blocked, so we couldn't go anywhere. The weather was terrible, and the whole plan just fell apart. And then, since we couldn't travel, we decided to stay home and try to make the best of it. We cooked some special food together, watched movies in the living room, and just relaxed as a family. Honestly, I felt pretty disappointed at first because I had waited so long for that trip, but I also felt grateful that we still had a good time together. So yeah, even though the holiday didn't go as planned, it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Have you had any memorable experiences involving a hospital visit? Explain what happened and why it stands out in your memory.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a really memorable hospital visit with my mom because I had a terrible cold. The hospital was pretty big and, honestly, it seemed a little scary to me back then. There were a lot of people waiting, so we sat for quite a long time before my name was called. Actually, once it was my turn, the doctor checked me and said I needed a shot, which made me really nervous. And then, when the nurse came over, she noticed I was scared and gently held my hand while talking to me in a calm voice. Honestly, I felt so much better because of her kindness, and I started to feel a little braver. In the end, that experience stood out because someone I didn’t even know made such a big difference in how I felt. So yeah, it’s still one of my favorite memories from my childhood.

question: People often have memories of special, beautiful or interesting hotels. Tell me about a hotel that you remember for some reason. Where was it? What did it look like? Describe it for me in as much detail as possible.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I stayed at a hotel with my family during a summer trip to the beach because we wanted to relax by the ocean. The hotel was right next to the water, and the view from our room was just beautiful. Actually, the lobby was really big and bright, and everything felt clean and welcoming, so it put us in a good mood right away. And then, every evening, we sat out on the balcony together, just watching the waves and talking about our day. We even ordered some snacks to the room and just enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere. Honestly, I felt so calm and happy there, and I remember thinking I never wanted to leave. So yeah, that hotel made our whole trip feel extra special, and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Sometimes, surprising or unexpected things can happen when you stay at a hotel. Tell me about a memorable experience you had while staying at a hotel. Maybe there was a problem with your room.
answer: Well, a while back, I stayed at a hotel with my family during a short weekend trip. We had been on the road all day, so we were really tired and just wanted to relax. When we finally got to our room, we actually found that it had been double-booked by mistake, and there were already other people inside. And then, since it was already late at night, we all felt pretty annoyed and a little embarrassed. I went down to the front desk and explained what happened, and the staff apologized right away. Honestly, I felt frustrated at first, but they surprised us by giving us a much nicer room for free. The new room was bigger and had a great view, so we ended up having a much better stay than we expected. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite hotel memories because something stressful turned into something really lucky for us.

question: Have you ever had a problem regarding traveling outside of your country for work or pleasure? Explain all the details about the situation.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I traveled abroad with a close friend because we wanted to explore a new country together. When we landed, we were really excited to start our adventure, but actually, something went wrong at the airport. One of our bags didn't show up on the baggage carousel, and we realized it was missing. The airport was pretty busy, so it took us a while to find the help desk. And then, we reported the missing bag and gave them all the details. Honestly, I felt really stressed and worried at that moment because all my clothes and important things were in that suitcase. In the end, the airport staff found the bag and delivered it to our hotel the next day, so everything worked out. So yeah, it's still one of my most memorable travel experiences because it taught me to stay calm when things go wrong.

question: Think about a time when your travel plans did not work out as expected. What happened? Tell me all about the circumstances, what you and the others did, and how the situation was finally resolved.
answer: Well, a while back, I went on a trip with my family, and things really didn’t go as planned. We were supposed to fly out for a holiday, so we got up early, packed everything, and arrived at the airport feeling excited. Actually, everything seemed fine until the airline suddenly announced that our flight was cancelled because of bad weather. The airport was crowded and noisy, and we had no idea what to do next. And then, we talked to the airline staff and managed to book a flight for the next day, but we had to find a place to stay for the night. Honestly, I felt so disappointed and stressed at first because we had planned everything carefully. In the end, we stayed at a nearby hotel and tried to make the best of it, and the trip still turned out great. So yeah, it’s still one of my most memorable travel experiences because it taught me to stay calm and flexible when things go wrong.

question: Have you ever forgotten something somewhere or could not find something important? Tell me in detail about an experience you had when you've lost or forgotten something important.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I lost my phone while I was out shopping with a friend because we had a free afternoon. Actually, I was carrying too many bags and wasn't really paying attention to my stuff. After we visited a few stores, I suddenly realized my phone was missing from my pocket. I started to panic since all my photos and contacts were on it, and I really needed it. And then, my friend quickly called my number, and to my surprise, a worker at one of the stores answered and said they had found my phone. We rushed back to the store, and the worker handed it to me from behind the counter. Honestly, I felt so relieved and grateful at that moment. So yeah, it's still one of my most unforgettable memories because I almost lost something so important but got it back thanks to a kind stranger.

question: Was there ever a time when the weather was really not what you had expected? Tell me in detail about a time when the weather was not what people had expected.
answer: Well, a while back, I had a day when the weather really surprised me. I was with some friends at a park because we had planned a picnic after seeing a sunny forecast. Actually, we were all excited and brought a lot of food, so we set up our mats and got ready to eat. The park was pretty crowded and everyone seemed to be enjoying the nice weather. And then, out of nowhere, dark clouds rolled in and it suddenly started pouring rain. We couldn't believe it because there had been no sign of rain at all earlier. Honestly, I felt shocked and a little disappointed at first, but we quickly grabbed our things and ran to a small shelter nearby. In the end, we ate our picnic under the roof and laughed about the whole situation, and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Tell me a specific story about something you did to prepare for your work or career. Tell me specifically what happened from beginning to end in as much detail as you can.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had an important job interview coming up, so I spent about a week preparing for it. I went to a quiet cafe near my house almost every day because it helped me focus better than at home. Actually, I brought a notebook and wrote down all the possible questions they might ask in the interview. And then, I practiced my answers out loud, repeating them until I felt more comfortable. My friend also joined me one evening, and we did a mock interview together, which really made a difference. Personally, I was pretty nervous the whole time because I really wanted that job. But after all that practice, I felt much more confident when the actual interview day arrived. So yeah, I ended up getting the job, and it's still one of my proudest memories.

question: When did you first become interesting in surfing the internet? Tell me about your first experience or experiences of surfing the internet in great detail. What was your first impression of it? What do you remember about it?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I first tried surfing the internet at home with my older brother. We had an old computer set up in the living room, and it was really slow compared to computers now. Actually, everything about it was new and kind of confusing for me, so my brother patiently showed me how to use it step by step. We searched for some simple games and watched a few funny videos together, and I remember waiting forever for each page to load. Honestly, I was so excited and amazed back then because it felt like I had discovered a whole new world. So yeah, that first experience really stuck with me, and it's still one of my favorite memories from my childhood.

question: Tell me about a project that you worked on that involved doing research on the internet. Start with giving me some background about the project. When and where was it? And then, tell me how you used the internet to get the project done.
answer: Well, a while back, I worked on a big school project with my friend, and we had to do a lot of research online. The project was about the environment, and we did most of it at home over almost two weeks. At first, I didn’t really know where to start, so I just opened my laptop and started searching for information. Actually, I read a bunch of articles, watched some videos, and saved all the useful things I found in one file. My friend and I split up the work, and we shared what we found by sending messages to each other. Personally, I felt pretty overwhelmed in the beginning because there was so much information out there. But after a while, I got used to it and things got easier once we had a plan. So yeah, we finished the project on time and even got a good grade, and it’s still one of my favorite memories from school.

question: We all have things that we remember on the internet. Talk about the memorable things that you have seen on the internet. Why were they memorable?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I came across a really touching video online late at night. I was lying in bed with my phone, just trying to relax before going to sleep. The video showed a man who rescued stray dogs every day, and the way he cared for them was really amazing. Actually, the place in the video looked a bit messy but full of happy dogs, and it felt so real. I watched the whole video from start to finish, and then I even read through all the comments because people were sharing their own stories. Personally, I was really moved by what I saw, and I almost teared up, which didn't happen to me very often. Honestly, I felt inspired and wanted to do something kind too, so I shared the video with my friends right away. So yeah, in the end, that video stuck with me, and it's still one of the most memorable things I've ever seen on the internet.

question: Describe something memorable you saw online for work or for leisure. Maybe it was impressive, unusual, humorous or meaningful for you. What did you notice about it? What made it special?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I saw an amazing photo online while I was taking a break at home. I had just been scrolling through some pictures for fun, and then I noticed this one shot of a tiny village up in the mountains. The place looked so peaceful and really beautiful, with little houses and lots of trees all around. Actually, the scene was so calm that I stopped scrolling and just stared at it for a long time. I even saved the photo on my phone because I liked it so much. Personally, I felt really relaxed and a bit inspired when I saw it. So yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories from being online, because that picture made me want to travel somewhere quiet like that someday.

question: Have you ever had a problem while you were surfing the internet? Tell me about the time when you ran into some kind of problem. Tell me everything that happened as well as what you did to fix the problem.
answer: Well, a while back, I was at home using the internet when my connection suddenly went out during an important video call for work. The screen froze, and then everything just stopped, which made me panic right away. Actually, I tried to fix it by restarting the router and waiting for a few minutes, but nothing changed. So I checked some cables and even tried to reconnect my computer, but it still didn't work. After that, I decided to call my internet company to see if they could help. They told me there was a big outage in my area, so there was nothing I could do but wait. Personally, I was really frustrated and worried because I didn't want to miss anything important from my meeting. In the end, the internet finally came back after about an hour, and I felt so relieved. So yeah, it's still a really memorable experience for me because I felt so helpless and stressed at the time.

question: How did you use the internet to get a project done in the past? What was the project about? Give me all the details.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had to make an important presentation for work, so I used the internet to get it done. I worked at my desk with my coworker, and we needed a lot of data and some good images for the slides. Actually, I spent a few days just searching for information online and downloading pictures that matched my topic. The office was pretty quiet back then, and I remember it felt a bit stressful because I wanted everything to look perfect. After I finished putting all the slides together, my coworker and I checked the presentation over a video call and fixed a few mistakes. Personally, I felt pretty tired and a little nervous because it took longer than I expected. In the end, the presentation went really well, and my boss liked it a lot, so yeah, it's still one of my favorite work memories.

question: Have you ever had to miss a class, a meeting or an interview at school or for work? Tell me the story of that experience. What happened? When did it happen and how did you resolve it?
answer: Well, a while back, I had to miss an important meeting at work because I woke up that morning feeling really sick with a high fever. I was supposed to meet my team at the office, but I could barely get out of bed, so I knew I couldn't make it. The place was usually pretty busy and everyone counted on each other, so I felt really bad about not being there. Actually, as soon as I realized how sick I was, I called my boss right away and explained everything honestly. And then, I sent her my notes by email so the team could still use my ideas even though I wasn't there. Personally, I felt so guilty and a little worried about letting everyone down, even though I couldn't help it. My boss understood and told me to just rest and take care of myself. So yeah, it's still a day I remember clearly because I really didn't want to miss that meeting, but I learned it's okay to ask for help when I need it.

question: Have you ever had an important appointment that you had to miss for some reason or perhaps you were late? Tell me in detail about an experience you had when you had trouble getting into an important appointment.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a really important doctor's appointment, and I almost missed it. I was supposed to see the doctor that morning, but I left my house a little late because I lost track of time. On my way there, the traffic was terrible and the bus barely moved at all. Actually, I started to get really worried, so I decided to get off the bus and run the rest of the way to the clinic. When I arrived, I was totally out of breath and sweating. Honestly, I felt so anxious because I really didn't want to miss my appointment. In the end, I made it just two minutes before my scheduled time, and the nurse was kind enough to tell me not to worry. So yeah, it's still one of those memories that remind me to always leave early for important things.

question: Have you ever had a time when you were supposed to visit family or friends, but you were not able to go? Tell me about a visit that you had to cancel for some reason. Tell me everything that happened.
answer: Well, a while back, I was supposed to visit my grandparents on the weekend, but I had to cancel at the last minute. I had really looked forward to seeing them because it had been a while since our last visit. But that morning, something urgent happened at work, so I had to go into the office instead. Actually, I called my grandmother as soon as I found out, and I apologized for not being able to come. She was so kind and told me not to worry, but I still felt really bad. Personally, I was pretty disappointed because I missed them a lot and had wanted to spend some time together. In the end, I did visit them the next weekend, but it's still a memory that stuck with me because I felt so sorry for canceling on them.

question: Was there a time when you had some free time and had some plans? But maybe the weather got bad. Or maybe you had work or chores that had to be taken care of. How did you deal with the situation that did not turn out as you had expected?
answer: Well, a few weeks ago, I had a free weekend and planned to go hiking with my friend. We had been looking forward to it for days because we both needed a break from work. But on that day, the weather suddenly got really bad and it started raining heavily, so we couldn't go hiking like we wanted. Actually, we felt pretty disappointed at first since we had really wanted to get some fresh air and enjoy nature. Instead, we decided to just stay at my place and watch some movies together. We ordered some food, talked for hours, and just relaxed inside while it rained outside. Personally, I was a little let down at first, but I ended up having a really good time just hanging out with my friend. So yeah, even though things didn't go as planned, it's still one of my favorite memories because we made the best of it and had a lot of fun anyway.

question: Have you ever bought tickets or had reservations and something went wrong? Tell me about the experience, with some background information about when and where this happened, and who you were with, and then tell me all the details of the incident and what you did to resolve it.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went to a movie theater with my girlfriend because we had booked tickets online for a weekend show. When we arrived at the theater, we went straight to the counter to pick up our tickets, but the staff couldn't find our reservation anywhere in their system. Actually, I showed them the confirmation message on my phone, but it still didn't show up on their computer. And then, the staff checked again and realized there was a system error on their side, so they apologized and quickly gave us new seats. Personally, I was really embarrassed and a bit annoyed at first because we were running late and I didn't want to miss the movie. In the end, we made it just in time, so it's still a pretty memorable experience for me because it turned out well even though it started off stressful.

question: Have you ever tried to watch a boring movie? If so, what did you do? Did you watch the whole movie? Tell me all the details of this experience.
answer: Well, a while back, I went to the theater with a couple of my friends to watch a movie that ended up being really boring. We chose that movie because the trailer had looked so exciting, so we all expected it to be great. When we got to the theater, the place was pretty crowded and the seats were comfortable, but as soon as the movie started, it was slow and the story just made no sense. Actually, I kept checking my phone because I was so bored, and at one point, I almost fell asleep in my seat. My friends looked just as bored as I was, and we kept glancing at each other and laughing quietly. Personally, I felt really disappointed because I had been looking forward to it. In the end, we watched the whole movie anyway just to see how it would finish, but it turned out to be a total waste of time. So yeah, it's still a funny memory for us and we sometimes joke about it even now.

question: Tell me about a situation you've had with some sort of equipment which broke or did not work properly. Give me the background of the situation and then tell me what happened, and how you resolved the problem.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a problem when my headphones suddenly broke while I was on the subway going to work. I was listening to music like I always did, and then, one side just stopped working out of nowhere. At first, I checked the cable and tried pressing all the buttons, but nothing helped. Actually, I even unplugged them and plugged them back in, but the sound still didn’t come back. Honestly, I was really frustrated because I used those headphones every single day and they were important to my morning routine. I ended up riding the rest of the way in complete silence, and it felt much longer than usual. Later that day, I went to a store and bought a new pair. So yeah, even though the new headphones were even better, I still remember that quiet and boring subway ride because it was such an annoying way to start my day.

question: Describe a time when you made a significant change to your diet or exercise routine. What led to this change? How did it work out? Describe what happened in detail from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a while back, I made a big change to my diet and daily routine. At that time, I was always feeling tired and unhealthy, and I ate a lot of fast food almost every day. Actually, I realized I needed to do something different, so I decided to stop eating junk food and started cooking simple meals at home. And then, I added a morning walk before work to my routine, which was something totally new for me. Honestly, the first week was pretty tough because I missed my old snacks and felt a bit tempted to go back. But after sticking with it, I started to feel lighter and much more energetic than before. So yeah, in the end, it became a real habit for me, and it's still one of the most memorable changes I've ever made to my life.

question: Have you ever had a memorable experience while visiting a park? Describe something interesting, fun, or unexpected that happened, and explain how you dealt with the situation.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I spent a really fun day at a park with some close friends. We went there because the weather was perfect and we just wanted to relax outside for a while. When we got to the park, the whole place was packed with people, and the grass looked so green and fresh under the bright sun. We picked a nice spot under a big tree, spread out a mat, and started eating snacks together. Actually, while we were chatting and laughing, a little dog suddenly ran over and grabbed one of our sandwiches. The dog's owner quickly came over and apologized, but we all just burst out laughing because the dog was so cute and sneaky. Honestly, I was really surprised at first but then I thought it was just so funny. So yeah, that unexpected moment made the day even more memorable, and it's still one of my favorite memories from that time.

question: Can you tell me about a memorable visit you made to your family or friends? Pick one of those special visits and tell me all about it. Start with some background about where and when it took place. And then, tell me the whole story of what happened during the visit.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I visited my old friend who lived in another city. It was during a long holiday, so I had some free time and decided to travel there. We hadn’t seen each other in years, so I was really looking forward to it. When I arrived by train, he picked me up at the station with a big smile on his face. Actually, the city was new to me, and everything felt a little different, but I was happy to be there. We walked around his town, ate at his favorite restaurant, and talked for hours about our lives. And then, we even stayed up late just catching up and laughing about old memories. Honestly, I felt so comfortable, like no time had passed at all between us. So yeah, it was such a special visit, and it’s still one of my favorite memories.

question: Have you ever made plans for trip or a party but had to cancel at the last minute because of something that happened unexpectedly? Tell me everything about what had happened that prevented you from going.
answer: Well, a while back, I planned a weekend trip to the beach with my friends, but something unexpected happened. We had all been really excited about it because we hadn't seen each other in a long time. The night before we were supposed to leave, one of my friends suddenly got really sick. Actually, we called each other right away and talked about what we should do. In the end, we all agreed that it wouldn't feel right to go without him, so we decided to cancel the trip. Personally, I was really disappointed because we had been looking forward to it for weeks. We canceled the hotel reservation and got most of our money back, which was a relief. And then, instead of going to the beach, we all went to visit our sick friend and brought him some food. So yeah, even though we missed out on the trip, it's still a good memory for me because we stuck together as friends.

question: Tell me about a time when you really wanted to go to a party or a celebration, but for some reason you could not go. What happened? Tell me the whole story from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a few years ago, my best friend invited me to his big birthday party, but I couldn't go in the end. I had been really excited about it for weeks because all of our friends were planning to be there. The day before the party, I came down with a really bad flu, so I had to call him and let him know I wouldn't make it. Actually, the party was held at his house, and I heard it was decorated with balloons and lights everywhere. While everyone was having fun, I just stayed home in bed feeling miserable and left out. Honestly, I was so disappointed because I really wanted to celebrate with everyone. In the end, my friend video-called me during the party so I could say hi to everyone, and it's still one of my favorite memories because that small gesture made me feel included even though I couldn't be there.

question: Have you ever had plans or tickets, then found out that you could not attend the performance? When was this? What happened? Tell me everything about the situation and what you did to try to resolve it.
answer: Well, a while back, I bought tickets for a concert that I had really wanted to see for a long time. I was planning to go with my friend, and we were both so excited about it. But just a few days before the show, my boss told me I had to work overtime because of an urgent project. The concert was at a big venue downtown, and actually, the place was supposed to be packed with fans that night. I tried to find someone to take my place at work, but nobody could help me out. Honestly, I felt so disappointed and a little heartbroken because the tickets were not cheap at all. In the end, I gave my tickets to my friend, and he went instead and sent me some videos from the concert, which made me feel a bit better. So yeah, it's still a pretty memorable experience because I missed something I had really looked forward to, but at least my tickets didn't go to waste.

question: Tell me about one of the memorable experiences you had at a pharmacy. What happened from beginning to end? Tell me everything in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a pretty memorable experience at a pharmacy near my house. It was late in the evening, and I went there because I had a terrible headache that just wouldn't go away. The place was quiet and a little empty, which actually made me feel even worse at first. I told the pharmacist how I was feeling, and he listened very carefully to everything I said. And then, he didn't just give me some medicine—he also explained how I should rest and drink more water to recover faster. Honestly, I felt really cared for because he was so patient and kind to me. I remember he even checked on me the next time I visited, just to see if I was doing better. So yeah, that small act of kindness really stuck with me, and it's still one of my favorite memories from a pharmacy.

question: Tell me about a time when you had a problem using your phone. What was the problem and how did you get help to solve the situation?
answer: Well, a while back, I was at home one evening when my phone suddenly stopped working. The screen just went completely black, and I had no idea what to do. I tried pressing every button, but nothing happened, so I started to get really worried. Actually, all my photos and contacts were saved on my phone, so I felt kind of desperate at that moment. After a few minutes, I decided to ask my brother for help because he usually knew a lot about phones. He told me to hold down two buttons together for a few seconds, so I did what he said. Luckily, my phone turned back on after a moment, and everything seemed normal again. Honestly, I was so relieved because I really thought I lost everything. So yeah, it was pretty memorable for me because I learned to always back up my files after that day.

question: Can you tell me about a memorable phone conversation you had using your phone? Tell me about that story from beginning to end with all the details about why it was so memorable.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I got a phone call that really stood out to me. I was just at home relaxing after a long day at work when, out of the blue, my old friend suddenly called me. Actually, we hadn't talked in a really long time, so I was surprised to see his name pop up on my phone. When I answered, he told me the big news that he was getting married soon. And then, we started talking about all the fun memories we made together back in school. Personally, I felt so happy and a little bit emotional to hear his voice again after such a long time. In the end, that phone call brought back so many good memories, and it's still one of my favorite moments to think about.

question: How did you choose the phone that you currently use? How did you hear about it? Did you do research? Tell me everything you can remember about your experience of choosing your phone from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a while back, my old phone finally stopped working, so I needed to get a new one. I remember it was on a weekend, and I was with my friends when I started thinking about which phone to buy. There were so many choices at the time, and the stores were pretty busy, so I felt a bit overwhelmed. Actually, I spent a few days reading reviews online and watching some videos to compare different models. And then, I talked to my friends and asked what phones they had and if they liked them. Personally, I was really unsure and kept changing my mind, so I felt pretty stressed during the whole process. In the end, I went to the store, tried out a few phones in person, and finally chose the one that felt the most comfortable for me. So yeah, it's still a memorable experience because I put in a lot of effort and ended up with a phone that I really liked.

question: Have you ever had an issue or a problem with your cell phone? Tell me about one of those experiences and what the situation was. How did you get it resolved?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a problem with my cell phone when I accidentally dropped it on the floor while I was rushing out the door. The screen completely cracked, and it was really hard to use my phone because I could barely read anything. Actually, I felt pretty upset at myself for being so careless, and I really didn't want to spend money on fixing it. But I knew I had to get it repaired, so I took it to a repair shop near my office during my lunch break. The repairman checked the phone and told me he could fix it in about an hour, which was faster than I expected. Personally, I was a bit nervous about whether it would turn out okay. Honestly, I felt so relieved when I saw the screen looking brand new again. So yeah, it's still a memorable experience because it taught me to be much more careful with my phone after that.

question: Tell me about a time when you had difficulties using your phone while travelling or commuting.
answer: Well, a while back, I had a really tough time using my phone while I was commuting to an important meeting. I was on the subway by myself that morning because I needed to get to work early. Suddenly, my phone battery just died, even though it had looked full before I left home. The subway was pretty crowded and noisy, so it was hard to focus, and I couldn't check the directions or message my coworker at all. Actually, I started to panic a little and didn't know what to do. Honestly, I felt really lost and helpless because I used my phone for everything back then. After that, I ended up asking a stranger for help, and he kindly showed me which exit to take. In the end, I managed to get to my meeting on time, and it's still a memorable experience because it taught me to always carry a charger when I travel.

question: Have you ever had to set up a new phone? Tell me about a specific time when you had to do that. What happened during the process?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had to set up a new phone by myself at home after I bought it from the store. I had never done it alone before, so I felt a little nervous at first. The phone was really shiny and looked a bit complicated to me. Actually, I didn’t know how to move my old photos and contacts, so I carefully followed the steps on the screen one by one. And then, I downloaded all my apps again and signed into each account, which took a lot of time. Personally, I was pretty frustrated because it was harder than I expected. But when everything was finally finished, I felt really proud and relieved. So yeah, it’s still a clear memory for me because I managed to do everything on my own for the first time.
