question: Severe weather can often cause serious problems. Tell me about an experience you had when the weather created some kind of problems. Maybe roads were flooded, maybe schools and businesses were closed due to snow or snow or cold weather conditions. Tell me about an experience you had when severe weather created some kind of problem.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I experienced really heavy rain that caused a huge problem in my area. I was at home with my family during the summer rainy season, and the rain just didn’t stop for several days. Actually, the streets around my house became completely flooded, so it was impossible to go outside. Because of that, I had to cancel all my weekend plans and just stayed indoors. The neighborhood looked pretty messy and a little dangerous, with water everywhere and cars stuck on the road. And then, after a few days, the water finally went down, but some of the roads were still damaged and dirty. Honestly, I felt pretty bored and frustrated at first, but I tried to make the best of it by relaxing at home. In the end, everything returned to normal, but I still remember how scary and inconvenient that rain was, so it really stuck with me.

question: Can you tell me about an early job you had? What kind of work did you do, who did you work with, and what did you learn from that experience? Please describe it in detail.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had my first part-time job at a small cafe near my house with some friendly coworkers. I decided to work there because I wanted to earn some money during my school break. Actually, the cafe was always really busy, so the atmosphere felt a bit stressful and I needed to learn everything quickly. I made coffee, cleaned tables, and talked to all kinds of customers every day. My coworkers always supported me and helped whenever I made mistakes, which made a big difference. Honestly, I felt extremely nervous at first, but after a while, I started to enjoy the work and felt more confident. In the end, that job taught me how to work well with people and handle difficult situations, so it really stuck with me and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Talk about the first coffee shop you went to. What was special about that place? What do you remember most?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went to my first coffee shop with some close friends after school because we wanted a place to relax and chat. Actually, the cafe was pretty small and cozy, with big windows and soft music playing in the background, so it felt really warm and welcoming. We found a table by the window, ordered some sweet drinks, and just talked for hours about everything. And then, we laughed a lot and shared some funny stories, which made the time fly by. Personally, I felt so grown-up and happy just sitting there with my friends, enjoying the atmosphere. After that day, the cafe became our regular hangout spot for a while, and every visit felt special. In the end, what I remembered most was the smell of fresh coffee and all the laughter we shared. So yeah, that's why that first visit to a coffee shop really stuck with me and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Please tell me about a memorable experience you have had at a café. When was it? Who were you with? What made this experience special?
answer: Well, a while back, I spent a really special afternoon at a café with an old friend because we hadn’t seen each other in almost two years. The café was quiet and warm at that time, so it felt like the perfect place for us to catch up. We ordered some coffee and found a cozy corner to sit in. Actually, we ended up talking for almost three hours, sharing stories and laughing about old memories. And then, we shared a slice of cake and looked through old photos on our phones, which brought back even more memories. Honestly, I felt so happy and a little emotional to be together again after such a long time. In the end, it reminded me just how much I had missed her, and that’s why the experience really stuck with me. I still think about that day whenever I visit a café.

question: Tell me about the first time you went hiking? How old were you, and who taught you? How have you developed your hiking skills since then?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I still remember the first time I went hiking with my dad near our house because he wanted to show me how to enjoy the outdoors. Actually, the trail was pretty steep, so I got tired really quickly and kept needing to stop and catch my breath. My dad stayed right by my side, walked slowly with me, and even taught me how to breathe properly while hiking. And then, after a lot of effort, we finally reached the top of the mountain, and the view was just beautiful. Honestly, I felt so proud of myself and a little out of breath at the same time. In the end, that first hike made a big impression on me, and that's why it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: What made you start hiking as part of your routine? Describe any specific events or motivations that led you to include hiking in your daily or weekly activities.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I started hiking after I went through a really stressful period at work. At that time, I was always tired and needed something to help me relax. One weekend, a close friend invited me to climb a small mountain with her, so I decided to join her. Actually, the mountain trail was pretty steep and I got tired quickly, but the fresh air and the view from the top felt incredible. We took some photos together and just enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere for a while. Honestly, after that first hike, I felt so much calmer and healthier than before. I started going hiking almost every weekend after that, and it slowly became a regular part of my life. So yeah, I still remember that first climb because it really helped me change my routine and feel better about everything.

question: Tell me about the most interesting place you've ever visited for hiking. Where was it, and why was it so interesting? Who did you go with? What was the experience like?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went hiking with my close friends at a famous mountain in the countryside because we wanted to see the autumn leaves. Actually, the trail was covered with bright, colorful leaves, so it looked almost magical and made the whole place feel special. We hiked up slowly, took lots of photos together, and then stopped several times to enjoy the amazing view. And then, after a while, we finally reached a small temple at the top, where we rested and talked for a bit. Honestly, I felt so peaceful and amazed by the beautiful scenery and the quiet atmosphere around us. In the end, it was the most beautiful hike I ever experienced, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still think about it sometimes.

question: Have you ever injured yourself while hiking? What kind of injury was it? What do you do to avoid injuries, and what safety precautions do you take?
answer: Well, a while back, I went hiking with a close friend, and I ended up hurting my ankle. It happened because I wasn't paying enough attention and stepped right on a loose rock. Actually, the trail was a bit uneven that day, so it was pretty easy to trip if you weren't careful. Right after I twisted my ankle, I felt a really sharp pain and had to sit down for a minute to catch my breath. And then, my friend helped me stand up, and we slowly made our way back down the mountain together. When I got home, I put some ice on my ankle, and luckily, it wasn't too serious. Honestly, I felt a little embarrassed about getting hurt like that, but I also felt grateful for my friend's help. In the end, that experience taught me to always wear proper shoes and watch my steps while hiking, so it's still a pretty memorable lesson for me.

question: There are always problems which happen in any home. Things break, projects do not go as planned, or people you live with don't cooperate. Tell me about some problems or issues that have happened in your home.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I was with my family at the time, and it happened because the heater was really old and just stopped working one very cold night. Actually, the whole apartment became freezing, so we all had to wear extra clothes and use a lot of blankets just to stay warm. And then, the next morning, we called a repair person to fix it. The repairman came pretty quickly and managed to fix the heater in about an hour, so we finally got the heat back. Honestly, I was so cold and grumpy that night, but after everything was fixed, I felt incredibly relieved and happy. In the end, it turned out to be a small problem, but it really stuck with me because I’ll never forget how cold that night was and how glad I was when everything went back to normal.

question: Sometimes we want to change something in our home, maybe get new furniture or do some painting or decorating. Tell me about one change that you made in your home. Tell me why you decided to make that change and everything you had to do to make that change happen.
answer: Well, a while back, I decided to change my room by getting a new desk and some shelves because my old desk was too small and my room felt really messy. At that time, I was studying a lot, so I needed a better space to focus. Actually, I spent a few days looking online for furniture and finally picked out some simple pieces that matched my style. I ordered them, and when they arrived, I spent the whole weekend putting everything together and cleaning up my room. The place looked much cleaner and more organized after I finished. Honestly, I felt a little tired from all the work, but I also felt really satisfied when I saw the result. In the end, my room looked so much nicer and brighter, so yeah, that's why it really stuck with me. I still love my new desk and shelves, and it's still one of my favorite changes I've made at home.

question: Talk about a special memory you had with your family members at home. Maybe it was something funny, surprising, or important that happened. Tell me about that moment that stands out in your memory. Give me lots of details.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I spent a really special night at home with my whole family. It happened because it was the first snow of the year, so we all stayed in together. At that time, we were just relaxing in the living room, and then my mom suggested that we make some hot soup. Actually, we all cooked together, ate by the window, and watched the snow falling outside. After that, we played some board games and laughed a lot until it got pretty late. Honestly, I felt so warm and happy to be surrounded by everyone that night. In the end, it was such a simple but perfect evening, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still think about that snowy night whenever I see the first snow each year.

question: Pick one of those problems you just mentioned and explain to me everything that happened. Start with when it happened and anything that may have caused the problem. Then, explain in detail everything you did to resolve the problem.
answer: Well, a few years ago, my kitchen sink got completely clogged one evening after we had washed a ton of dishes from a big family dinner. I was with my family at home, and actually, the water just wouldn’t go down at all, so the sink quickly filled up and started to smell a little bad. I tried to fix it myself by pouring some hot water down the drain, but it didn’t really help. And then, I decided to call my dad for help, and he came over and showed me how to use a simple plunger to unclog the sink. Personally, I was pretty stressed and frustrated at first, but once the water finally went down, I felt really proud of myself for solving the problem. In the end, the sink worked perfectly again, and I learned a useful skill that day. So yeah, it’s still a small but memorable victory for me.

question: What was a memorable movie you watched in the past? What was it about? What was special about that movie? Would you recommend that movie to other people?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I watched a really memorable movie with my close friend at the theater. It was an emotional drama about family, so we didn't know what to expect. At that time, the theater was dark and quiet, so we got really into the story. Actually, as we watched, the story became more touching and we both got pulled in by the characters. We watched the whole thing, and by the end, we were both almost crying. After the movie, we sat in a nearby cafe for about an hour and talked about what we had just seen. Honestly, I felt so moved and a little surprised by how good it was. In the end, that movie really stuck with me, and that's why I still recommend it to everyone. I'll never forget how it made me feel.

question: Who is one of your favorite actors? Tell me a specific story about something this actor did that you heard about in the news. Begin the story with some details about the actor or the actress and then tell me all the details of what happened, especially things that made this incident so interesting to movie fans.
answer: Well, a while back, I read a really touching story about one of my favorite actors. He was a famous Korean actor that I had loved for years, and at that time, I saw in the news that he had quietly donated a lot of money to help children in need. Actually, the article explained that he didn't want anyone to know about his donation, so he tried to keep it a secret. But after a while, fans found out anyway, and everyone started praising him online for his kindness. Honestly, I felt so proud and happy to be a fan of someone like him, because he was not just talented but also had a good heart. In the end, it made me respect him even more, and that's why this story really stuck with me. I still think about it sometimes.

question: Could you think back to a particularly memorable time when you heard live music? Describe that experience in detail. What was it? Where were you? Who were you with? Who did you hear? What happened? And what happened that made that performance so memorable or special?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went to my first live concert with my best friends because our favorite band was performing in our city. The venue was really huge and packed with fans, so the energy felt incredible that night. Actually, when the band finally came out on stage, everyone started cheering and we all jumped and sang along to every song. And then, during the last song, the whole crowd lit up their phones and waved them in the air, which looked amazing. Honestly, I felt so excited and a little emotional in that moment because it was something I had always dreamed of experiencing. In the end, it was the best concert I ever went to, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still get chills thinking about it.

question: Describe for me your first trip to another country. When was the trip? Where did you go? Who did you go with and what did you do? Describe that experience for me in as much detail as possible.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went on my first trip to another country with my family. We traveled to Japan because we had always wanted to see what it was like there. Actually, when we arrived, everything seemed so new and exciting, and the streets were full of people and bright lights, which made the whole place feel lively. We walked around the city, tried lots of delicious Japanese food, and visited some beautiful old temples together. And then, we even rode the famous bullet train, which was a really cool experience for all of us. Honestly, I felt amazed and a little nervous at first, but the more we explored, the happier I became. In the end, that trip was really special to me because it was my first time experiencing another culture with my family, so yeah, it's still one of my favorite memories.

topic: 여행 중 가장 기억에 남는 경험
question: What is the most memorable experience you've had while traveling? When and where did you go? What happened, and why was it unforgettable? Provide as much detail as possible.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I traveled to another country with my friends and had one of the most memorable experiences of my life. We had spent the whole day walking around and exploring, so by the evening, we were pretty tired. Actually, we decided to go to a quiet beach to relax and watch the sunset together. The beach was almost empty, and the sound of the waves made the place feel really peaceful. We just sat on the sand, watched the sky slowly turn orange and pink, and didn't say much at all. And then, we took some photos to try to remember the moment, but honestly, the pictures couldn't capture how amazing it really was. Personally, I felt so calm and grateful just being there with my friends. So yeah, that sunset was the most beautiful thing I saw on that trip, and it's still one of my favorite memories.

question: Sometimes, something out of the ordinary happens while traveling. I wonder if you have ever experienced anything surprising, unexpected or unusual while on a trip. Tell me about the story of that experience in detail. Start by telling me when and where you were traveling. And then, tell me all the details of that experience, especially what happened that made it so unforgettable.
answer: Well, a while back, I was traveling abroad with a friend when something really unexpected happened. We were exploring an old town, just enjoying the sights, when suddenly it started pouring rain and we didn’t have an umbrella. Actually, we tried to find somewhere to hide, but the streets were pretty empty and there wasn’t much shelter around. So we ended up running into a tiny local shop, and the owner noticed we were soaked and offered us some hot tea. And then, we sat there talking with him for almost an hour while we waited for the rain to stop. Honestly, I felt so surprised and touched by his kindness to complete strangers. In the end, that little moment became my favorite part of the trip, and that’s why it really stuck with me. I still smile thinking about it.

question: Tell me a specific story about a time that you or someone you know travelled outside of your country. Perhaps, something happened that was funny, interesting, frightening, etc. Tell me the story of what happened from beginning to end.
answer: Well, a few years ago, my friend had a really funny experience when she traveled outside our country. She went to a small restaurant by herself because she wanted to try some local food. Actually, she didn't understand the menu, so she just pointed at a random dish and hoped for the best. When the food arrived, it turned out to be a huge plate, much bigger than she expected, and the whole restaurant seemed to notice. She tried hard to finish it, but she couldn't even eat half of it. Honestly, she felt so embarrassed but also couldn't stop laughing at the situation. In the end, the kind staff packed up the leftovers for her to take home, and we still laugh about it whenever we talk. So yeah, that's why it really stuck with both of us, and it's still one of our favorite travel memories.

question: How you became interested in going to parks) How did you first become interested in going to parks? When and where did you go? Who did you go with? How has your interest in going to parks changed over time?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I first became interested in going to parks after a really busy week with work. At that time, I was feeling pretty stressed, and my close friend suggested that we go for a walk in a big park near our neighborhood. Actually, the park was really quiet and peaceful, and there were lots of trees and open spaces, which made it feel so different from the busy city. We walked along the path, talked about everything, and just enjoyed the fresh air together. And then, after that day, I started going to the park almost every weekend by myself because it helped me unwind. Honestly, I felt a lot calmer and happier every time I spent time there. In the end, that first peaceful walk really changed how I spent my weekends, and it's still one of my favorite memories whenever I think about relaxing.

question: Memorable experience in a park) Tell me about a memorable or unforgettable experience you had at a park. Who were you with, and what did you do? Was there a special event or did something unexpected happen?
answer: Well, a while back, I had a really memorable day at a park with my family because there was a small outdoor festival going on. The park was packed with people, music, and food stalls, so it felt super lively and exciting. Actually, we walked around together, tried some delicious street food, and listened to a live band that was playing nearby. After that, we found a nice spot on the grass and just relaxed in the sun for a while. And then, my little brother joined some kids playing frisbee, which made us all laugh. Honestly, I felt so happy and surprised by the fun atmosphere and how much we enjoyed such a simple day. In the end, it turned an ordinary walk into a really special memory, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still think about that afternoon sometimes.

question: Sometimes, funny or unexpected things happen while shopping. Tell me the story of a memorable shopping experience you had. Maybe you saw a friend at a store, maybe the item you bought was broken or did not work. Tell me in detail about a shopping trip that you still remember for some reason.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I had a pretty funny shopping experience at a big department store with the goal of buying a simple gift for my mom. Actually, the store was packed that day because there was a huge sale, so it felt really crowded and a bit overwhelming. While I was looking at some scarves, I suddenly bumped into my old coworker from my previous job. And then, we both laughed because we hadn't seen each other in so long, and we decided to walk around the store together. After shopping for a while, we grabbed some coffee in the food court and caught up about our lives. Personally, I felt so happy and surprised to meet her by chance after such a long time. In the end, I not only found a nice gift for my mom but also reconnected with an old friend, so it's still one of my favorite memories when I think about shopping.

question: You have probably had many interesting experiences while shopping, some good, some funny, some problematic. Tell me about one particular shopping experience with as much detail as possible, where you were, what happened, how it happened, what you and/or others did, and how it all turned out.
answer: Well, a while back, I had a really memorable shopping experience at a small local market with myself because I wanted to buy some fresh fruit for the weekend. The market was pretty crowded and full of friendly vendors, so it felt really lively and welcoming. Actually, as I was checking out some apples, one kind vendor offered me a free sample to try, which was really nice. And then, we started chatting for a while, and she even gave me a small discount on the fruit I picked out. Honestly, I felt so warm and happy because of her kindness and the friendly atmosphere. In the end, I went home with some delicious fruit and a big smile on my face, and that's why that shopping trip really stuck with me. I still think about it whenever I visit that market.

question: Traveling can lead to many kinds of interesting, funny and unexpected experiences. Tell me about one travel experience you had that was unforgettable. Start by telling me when this happened, where you were, and who you were with. Then, tell me about all the things that happened to make that experience so unforgettable.
answer: Well, a few years ago, I went on an unforgettable trip to the countryside with my close friends because we wanted to escape the city and spend a quiet weekend together. The area was surrounded by green hills, so it felt really calm and fresh at that time. Actually, we cooked food outside, sat around a small fire, and talked late into the night, which made the atmosphere even more special. And then, the next morning, we all woke up early to watch the sunrise over the hills, and the view was just amazing. Honestly, I felt so relaxed and really connected with my friends during those moments. In the end, it was such a peaceful and special trip, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still think about that quiet morning.

topic: 집에서 보낸 휴가 중 특별한 경험
question: Could you tell me about an unusual, unexpected, or satisfying experience you had while at home on vacation? Tell me all the details of that experience. What happened? Who was involved? And why was that experience so memorable?
answer: Well, a few years ago, I spent my vacation just staying at home by myself because I was feeling too tired to travel anywhere. Actually, at that time, I had the entire place to myself, so it felt really peaceful and quiet. I slept in every morning, cooked some delicious meals, and watched a bunch of movies that I had missed before. And then, I even found time to clean and reorganize my room, which made the space feel totally fresh. Honestly, I felt so relaxed and recharged by the end of that week. In the end, it turned out to be a much better vacation than I expected, and that's why it really stuck with me. I still remember how satisfied I felt after that simple break at home.

question: Tell me about an appointment you remember from your childhood. Maybe you visited a doctor, the dentist, or a new school. Tell me about the place you went to for this appointment. What was your impression? How did you feel?
answer: Well, when I was a kid, I went to the dentist with my mom for a check-up because one of my teeth really hurt. The place was small and quiet, but honestly, it still seemed a little scary to me back then. There were a few other kids waiting, so we had to sit for a while before my turn came. When it was finally my turn, the dentist checked my teeth and told me it was nothing serious. Actually, he was really kind and gentle, so I calmed down after a few minutes. At first, I felt nervous and worried, but I started to feel much better as the appointment went on. Personally, I remember feeling so relieved when it was all over. In the end, it's still one of my most memorable childhood experiences because it helped me get over my fear of the dentist.

question: Tell me about an early memory you had when you visited friends or family. Describe your impression for me. Where did you go? Who did you visit? What was the experience like?
answer: Well, when I was young, I went to my grandparents' house with my parents for the holidays because our whole family always got together at that time. The house was old but really cozy, so it always felt warm and welcoming whenever we visited. My grandmother cooked a huge dinner, and we all gathered around one big table to eat together. Actually, we spent hours talking and laughing, and after dinner, all the kids went outside to play while the adults chatted in the living room. I remember I felt really happy and comfortable there, just enjoying being with my family. Honestly, those moments made me feel like I truly belonged. In the end, it's still one of my favorite childhood memories, and I think about it whenever I miss my family.

question: Tell me about a memory you have about a special food or a dish from your childhood. Tell me where and when you had this dish. Describe in detail. What made this experience so memorable?
answer: Well, when I was little, I had a really special dish at my grandmother's house during a holiday with my family because we always gathered there for special occasions. The kitchen was filled with amazing smells, and the table was always packed with all kinds of food. My grandmother cooked a big pot of soup that she only made on holidays, so it felt like something really important. We all sat down together, and I tried the soup for the first time, and actually, it tasted so good that I couldn't help but ask for a second bowl. Everyone started laughing at me, and honestly, I felt so warm and happy sitting there with my family. In the end, it's still one of my favorite memories, and I’ll never forget that taste. That’s why this experience was so memorable for me.

question: I'd like you to pick a favorite place in your country from your childhood and describe that place for me. What are your memories of that place? What was it like when you were a child?
answer: Well, when I was young, my favorite place was a small beach near my hometown, and I often went there with my family because it was close and easy to get to. The beach was really beautiful back then, and the water was so clear that I just wanted to stay there all day. There weren’t many people around, so it felt calm and peaceful. We usually walked along the sand, looked for shells, and watched the waves for hours. Actually, after walking around, we always sat down together and enjoyed some snacks while listening to the sound of the ocean. I remember I felt so free and relaxed every time I visited. Honestly, those moments made me really happy because I could spend quality time with my family without any worries. In the end, it’s still one of my favorite childhood places, and I still think about it sometimes, so that’s why it means so much to me.

question: Describe for me an early memory that you have of your country's geography. Perhaps, you visited a special place or went to an important natural landmark. Describe what you saw when you visited this special place.
answer: Well, when I was a kid, I went to a famous mountain with my whole family because my parents wanted to take a short trip during the summer. The mountain was huge, and it was covered with green trees everywhere, so it looked really fresh and beautiful. The air was clean and cool, so I felt calm just standing there and taking it all in. We walked up a small trail together, took some photos, and enjoyed the amazing view from the top. Actually, after that, we had a simple lunch on a flat rock, which made the experience even more special. I felt so small but really peaceful at the same time, surrounded by nature. Honestly, it was a moment that made me appreciate the beauty of my country. In the end, it's still one of my favorite memories, and that's why it stuck with me so much.

question: Describe for me a special holiday memory that you have from your childhood. Tell me where you were and what the place looked like and everything you remember from the holiday scene.
answer: Well, when I was young, I spent a special holiday at my grandparents' house with my whole family because everyone always got together for the big holidays. The house was warm and bright, and the table was covered with all kinds of delicious food, so it really felt festive. There were so many relatives around, and the place was loud and full of laughter. We all ate a big meal together, and then the kids got some pocket money and played games in the living room. Actually, after that, we all sat around and listened to the older people tell funny stories from the past, which made everyone laugh even more. I felt so happy and excited the whole time, and honestly, I just loved being surrounded by everyone I cared about. In the end, it's still one of my best holiday memories, and I don't think I'll ever forget those days.

question: Do you remember visiting a hospital as a child? Describe any memorable experiences you had and how you felt at the time.
answer: Well, when I was a kid, I went to a hospital with my mom because I had a really bad cold and a high fever. The hospital was pretty big and, honestly, it seemed a little scary to me back then. There were so many people waiting, so we had to sit for a long time before my turn came. Actually, after waiting, the doctor finally checked me and said I needed a shot, which made me really nervous. The nurse noticed I was scared, so she held my hand and talked to me the whole time, trying to make me feel better. I was really frightened at first, but after the shot, I started to feel much better and was relieved it was over. Personally, I felt so grateful for the nurse’s kindness because it made a big difference. In the end, that experience stayed with me, and it’s still one of my most memorable childhood memories because of how caring the nurse was.

question: Tell me what recycling was like when you were a child. Was there a particular place to throw away the recyclables? Did you have special containers? Describe what it was like and what you did in detail.
answer: Well, when I was young, recycling was already a normal part of my daily routine at home with my family. We always sorted our trash because it was just something every household did back then. There were a few different bins in our house—one for paper, one for plastic, and another for cans. Near our apartment building, there was a special area where everyone in the neighborhood took their recyclables. Actually, every week, my mom asked me to carry the recycling bags down and sort them into the correct bins outside. And then, I double-checked to make sure everything was in the right place before going back home. Honestly, I felt a little proud whenever I helped out because it made me feel more responsible, almost like an adult. Overall, it's still a small but meaningful memory for me, and I think that's why I still try to recycle properly even now.

question: Think about another country that you visited when you were young. Describe what this other country was like with lots of details. What were your impressions of that place?
answer: Well, when I was young, I visited another country with my family for a short trip because my parents wanted to go somewhere new during our vacation. The city we went to was really beautiful, with lots of old buildings and narrow streets, so it felt charming and very different from my hometown. Actually, the place was really clean and not too crowded, so it was pretty comfortable to walk around and explore. We spent most of our time just looking around the streets, trying some local food, and taking a lot of photos together. And then, after we finished eating, we just walked slowly and enjoyed the new atmosphere. I felt so excited at that time because everything seemed so new and interesting to me. Honestly, it was a really special experience for me since I didn’t travel abroad very often back then. In the end, it's still one of my favorite memories, and I still think about that trip sometimes because it made me want to see more of the world.

question: Tell me about your early memories of shopping and how you came to enjoy it so much. You might include information about where and when you used to shop, the people you went shopping with, what kinds of things you used to do while shopping or anything else that would give a clear picture of that experience.
answer: Well, when I was a kid, I used to go shopping with my mom almost every weekend because she always took me along when she ran errands. We usually went to a big store near our house, and it was always bright and full of interesting things to look at. question: I'd like to give you a situation and ask you to act it out. You want to open a bank account. Call the bank and ask three to four questions about the services they offer to open an account.

question: I'd like to give you a situation and ask you to act it out. You need to open a new bank account. Go to the bank and ask the bank representative three or four questions to learn everything about what you need to do to open an account.

question: I'd like to give you a situation and ask you to act it out. You want to borrow a bike from your friend to go to a shopping mall and buy something. Call your friend and ask three or four questions as to whether you can borrow the bike.

question: I'd like to give you a situation and ask you to act it out. You are driving your car through a small town and have car trouble. Go to the nearest service station and ask for help. Ask three or four questions in order to get the information you need to get your car fixed.

Can you take a look at my car soon? Do I need to make an appointment, or can I just bring it in? I'm not sure what the problem is, so can you check it and tell me what's wrong? Is it something you can fix here? If you can fix it, how long do you think it will take? Is it possible to get it done today, or will I need to leave my car overnight? Also, could you let me know how much it might cost to repair? Do you accept credit cards, or is it cash only? While I wait, is there a waiting area at your station? Or maybe a cafe nearby where I can sit for a bit? Is there anything I need to bring or prepare before I come in? Thank you so much for your help!
